title: Writing - Part 2: Exercise 7
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 220-260 words.

tasks.title: Report
tasks.prompt: You have been asked by the local tourist board to write a report on a local festival that took place last month. Describe the event, evaluate its success, and suggest how it could be improved for next year.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Report on the Riverfront Arts Festival

Introduction
The purpose of this report is to assess the success of the annual Riverfront Arts Festival held last month and to provide recommendations for future iterations. The evaluation is based on attendee feedback and local media coverage.

Description of the Event
The festival spanned three days and featured a diverse range of activities, including live jazz performances, outdoor theatre, and an artisanal food market. The primary objective was to showcase local talent and attract visitors to the historic district. Attendance exceeded expectations, with an estimated 5,000 visitors over the weekend.

Evaluation of Success
The event was largely successful in its mission. The quality of the musical acts was praised for its professionalism, and the food market provided a significant boost to local vendors. Furthermore, the festival successfully fostered a sense of community pride. However, the unexpected volume of visitors led to significant logistical challenges. The most frequent complaints concerned the lack of adequate public conveniences and the insufficient number of waste disposal points, which resulted in littering by Sunday evening.

Recommendations for Improvement
To ensure the festival’s continued growth, I suggest:
- Increasing the number of temporary toilet facilities by at least 50%.
- Partnering with a professional waste management company to ensure regular collection during the event.
- Implementing a digital ticketing system for popular performances to manage crowd density more effectively.

Conclusion
The Riverfront Arts Festival has the potential to become a major regional attraction. By addressing these infrastructural shortcomings, the board can ensure a more seamless experience for all participants next year.

tasks.title: Email
tasks.prompt: A friend from an English-speaking country is applying for a job as a tour guide in your city. Write an email giving them advice on what skills they should highlight and what local knowledge they should prepare for the interview.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Hi Mark,

It was great to hear that you're going for the tour guide position! I think you'd be a natural fit for the role, but as you know, our city’s tourism board is quite rigorous with their selection process. Here’s some advice to help you nail the interview.

First and foremost, you should highlight your interpersonal skills. They aren't just looking for a walking encyclopaedia; they want someone who can manage diverse groups and keep people engaged for hours. Mention your experience in public speaking or any customer-facing roles you’ve held. Emphasize your ability to adapt your tone—guiding a group of rowdy teenagers is very different from leading a historical society!

Regarding local knowledge, don't just stick to the dates of the main monuments. Everyone knows when the cathedral was built. Instead, prepare some 'hidden' stories—anecdotes about local legends, the best-kept secrets for authentic tapas, or how the city changed during the industrial revolution. Showing that you understand the soul of the city, not just its facts, will really set you apart.

Also, be prepared for questions on conflict resolution. They might ask how you’d handle a difficult guest or an unexpected delay in the schedule. Show them you're calm, resourceful, and always have a 'Plan B'.

Best of luck with the preparation! Let me know if you want to do a mock tour around the old town this weekend.

Cheers,
Santi

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt: You have seen this announcement on a website for professionals: 'We want reviews of co-working spaces. Tell us about the facilities, the atmosphere, and whether it’s a good environment for networking.' Write your review.
tasks.modelAnswer:
The Hive: More Than Just a Desk

Finding the right workspace is crucial for any freelancer, and after trying several locations in the city, I recently spent a month at 'The Hive'. Located in a beautifully renovated textile mill, it offers a sophisticated alternative to the traditional home office.

In terms of facilities, The Hive is second to none. The ergonomic chairs and height-adjustable desks are a godsend for long working days. There are also several soundproof 'phone booths' for private calls, which is a feature many other spaces overlook. The high-speed internet is exceptionally reliable, and the communal kitchen is stocked with high-quality coffee and healthy snacks, which is a thoughtful touch.

The atmosphere is best described as 'focused yet vibrant'. Unlike some co-working spaces that can feel like quiet libraries, there is a gentle hum of activity here that is motivating rather than distracting. The industrial-chic aesthetic, with plenty of natural light and indoor plants, creates an environment where you actually want to spend your time.

However, where The Hive truly excels is networking. They host weekly 'Lunch and Learn' sessions and evening mixers specifically designed to connect members. I’ve already collaborated with a web designer I met in the lounge, which has been invaluable for my business. It’s a community of ambitious professionals rather than just a collection of individuals.

It is slightly more expensive than its competitors, but the networking opportunities and top-tier facilities more than justify the cost. If you're looking to elevate your professional life, I highly recommend giving it a try.
